attacking_style: Pure power sprinter who finishes in chaotic, disorganized sprints. Capable of winning from disadvantaged positions in final kilometer through raw speed rather than optimal positioning.
team_tactics: Demonstrated ability to win without proper leadout support, suggesting either team weakness in final positioning or individual capacity to operate independently in sprint chaos.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint stages where pure power can overcome positioning deficits. Thrives in disorganized finishes where traditional leadout trains break down.
vulnerability: Reliance on pure power suggests potential weakness when facing organized leadout trains on technical finishes requiring perfect positioning rather than raw speed.
key_patterns: Wins from poor positioning through power, not optimal sprint setup or leadout.
Capitalizes on chaotic sprint scenarios where traditional leadout advantages dissolve.
Operates effectively in isolation without team support in final kilometers.
form_trajectory: unknown: single observation from 2017, insufficient recent data to assess current trajectory
